text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST AND MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 55-year-old female with known metastatic melanoma, BRAF V600E-mutated, previously treated with dabrafenib/trametinib, subsequent ipilimumab/nivolumab (complicated by immune-mediated colitis requiring hospitalization), now on nivolumab monotherapy maintenance. Stereotactic radiosurgery to brain metastases previously performed. Restaging evaluation.

---

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the uneventful administration of intravenous iohexol contrast. Coronal and sagittal reformats reviewed. Comparison made to prior CT dated approximately 12 weeks ago.

FINDINGS:

Chest:
Lungs: Several subcentimeter pulmonary micronodules are again identified, essentially unchanged in size and distribution. The largest measures approximately 4 mm in the right lower lobe and has been present on multiple prior examinations. These remain nonspecific and indeterminate but are stable in appearance and favored to be benign. No new pulmonary nodules or masses. No consolidation or pleural effusion. No pneumothorax.

Mediastinum/Hila: No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. The heart is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. Aorta and great vessels appear unremarkable.

Chest wall/Soft tissues: No suspicious soft tissue masses or subcutaneous nodularity identified. Previously noted subcutaneous lesions are no longer visualized.

Abdomen:
Liver: Continued interval improvement compared to prior imaging. The dominant hepatic lesion in segment VI has decreased substantially and now measures approximately 1.8 cm in greatest dimension (previously measured approximately 3.4 cm on the study from approximately 3 months ago). Several additional smaller hepatic lesions that were previously noted are no longer clearly visualized on the current study. No new hepatic lesions identified. No biliary ductal dilatation. Portal vein patent.

Spleen: Normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. No focal lesions.

Pancreas: Unremarkable. No ductal dilatation or peripancreatic stranding.

Adrenal glands: No focal adrenal lesions.

Kidneys: Both kidneys demonstrate normal enhancement. No hydronephrosis or suspicious renal masses.

Gastrointestinal: No bowel obstruction. No free intraperitoneal fluid or gas. Appendix not separately visualized but no periappendiceal inflammatory changes.

Retroperitoneum/Lymph nodes: No retroperitoneal or mesenteric lymphadenopathy. Previously described lymphadenopathy at the porta hepatis and along the celiac axis has resolved bilaterally.

Pelvis:
No pelvic lymphadenopathy. Uterus and adnexa unremarkable for patient age. Bladder is adequately distended and unremarkable. No pelvic free fluid.

Bones: Visualized osseous structures without suspicious lytic or blastic lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine noted.

Soft tissues: No new subcutaneous or intramuscular nodules.

---

MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST

TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the brain obtained with and without intravenous gadolinium contrast, including T1, T2, FLAIR, DWI, and post-contrast T1 sequences. Comparison made to prior MRI brain obtained approximately 3 months prior.

FINDINGS:

Brain parenchyma: No new areas of abnormal signal or enhancement identified. White matter signal changes are mild and nonspecific.

Previously treated lesions: The right frontal and left cerebellar lesions that underwent stereotactic radiosurgery demonstrate continued treatment effect. The right frontal focus demonstrates a small area of T1 shortening consistent with post-treatment change/hemosiderin deposition, without associated enhancement or surrounding edema. The left cerebellar lesion similarly demonstrates post-SRS signal alteration without nodular or ring enhancement. No imaging features to suggest viable tumor at either site on this study.

No new enhancing lesions identified in the supra- or infratentorial compartments. No leptomeningeal enhancement. No midline shift. Ventricular system is normal in caliber. No hydrocephalus.

Sella and parasellar regions: Unremarkable. No pituitary lesions.

Vascular structures: Major intracranial vessels appear patent on the post-contrast sequences.

Orbit/skull base: No suspicious osseous or soft tissue lesions at the skull base.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Continued interval improvement in hepatic metastatic disease compared to prior imaging. The dominant hepatic lesion has decreased substantially in size. Several smaller hepatic lesions that were previously noted are no longer visualized.

2. Resolution of previously identified bilateral lymphadenopathy. No subcutaneous disease identified.

3. Stable bilateral pulmonary micronodules, favored benign/inflammatory; no new pulmonary lesions.

4. MRI brain: No new intracranial lesions. Previously treated right frontal and left cerebellar lesions demonstrate post-SRS treatment changes without imaging evidence of viable tumor. Findings are stable to mildly improved compared to prior.

5. No new sites of disease identified.

Correlate with clinical assessment and tumor markers as clinically indicated.

Reporting Radiologist: [Electronically signed]
Department of Radiology
